_comment: Representative food-log fixtures for the FTY-256 local dogfood smoke.
These are the SUBMITTED USER INPUTS the smoke sends to the live local API
plus the outcome the estimator must deliver — they are verification data,
not estimator behavior. Kept as data (not a Python literal) so the smoke
code stays generic: it special-cases nothing and hardcodes no brand phrase,
keeps holding over backend/app. Calorie bands are plausibility tolerances for
the verification, never a nutrition table the estimator reads.
expected_item_count: exact number of derived items, or null for 'at least one'.
forbid_source_types: source_type values that would be a WRONG resolution.
forbid_substrings: tokens that must not appear in an item name/label/ref.
expected_items: per-item plausibility bands for multi-item fixtures. Each
band's lowercase 'match' substring must locate a derived item (name/label/
ref), and every matched item's calories must sit inside [kcal_low,
kcal_high] — so a bad split cannot hide behind a passing total band.

fixtures.raw_text: two scrambled eggs and one slice buttered toast
4 toppables brand crackers with 1tbsp of loblaws store brand (PC/presidents choice) dill pickle hummus
tuna salad sandwich on white bread, about 1/2 a can of tuna
made good mornings - chocolate chip organic soft baked oat bars, 1 serving
